notes.text: To expand a single bracket, multiply every term inside the bracket by the term outside: $a(b + c) = ab + ac$.
Multiply the outside term by each term inside the bracket, one at a time.
Fractions work the same way: $\frac{1}{2}(2x + 6) = x + 3$.
Do not forget the second term — writing $2x + 5$ instead of $2x + 10$ is a common slip.
Written out in full, the general rule is: $$a(b + c) = ab + ac$$

items: Add the results to get $ab + ac$.
